message:
fix(fsfs): eliminate TOCTOU race in sentinel and PID file acquisition

The previous sentinel lock and PID file acquisition logic had a
time-of-check-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race: it would first read/check for
an existing file, then separately write a new one. Under concurrent
contention (e.g., multiple fsfs daemon starts or concurrent pipeline
lock acquisitions), two processes could both see "no lock exists" and
both write their own sentinel, resulting in dual lock holders.

Replace the read-then-write pattern with atomic O_CREAT|O_EXCL
(OpenOptions::create_new) so exactly one process wins the file creation.
The loser sees AlreadyExists, then checks whether the existing lock is
stale (dead PID or exceeded timeout) and retries up to 2 times after
cleaning stale files.

concurrency.rs changes:
- Add write_sentinel_exclusive() using create_new(true) for atomic
  sentinel file creation
- Restructure try_acquire_sentinel() into a retry loop:
  attempt create_new -> on AlreadyExists, check liveness/staleness ->
  remove stale -> retry
- Add SENTINEL_ACQUIRE_MAX_RETRIES constant (2) bounding contention
- Return WouldBlock with contention message if retries exhausted

lifecycle.rs changes:
- Restructure PidFile::acquire() identically: atomic create_new attempt
  in a retry loop with stale cleanup
- Add PidFile::ACQUIRE_MAX_RETRIES constant (2)
- Return AddrInUse with contention message if retries exhausted

concurrency_stress.rs test additions:
- sentinel_atomic_acquire_has_single_winner_under_race: spawns 32
  threads behind a Barrier, verifies exactly 1 winner and 31 WouldBlock
  losers
- pid_file_atomic_acquire_has_single_winner_under_race: spawns 24
  threads behind a Barrier, verifies exactly 1 winner and 23 AddrInUse
  losers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
